name: Greek Keftethes ( Meatballs)
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Lemon juice or red wine
recipeInstructions:
1. In a skillet, heat half the oil and half the butter over medium heat.
2. Sauté onion until golden brown, then remove from the skillet and place in a bowl.
3. Add bread crumbs, 1/2 cup water, egg yolks, garlic, salt, oregano, and parsley to the bowl. Mix well.
4. Gradually add ground beef and mix until all ingredients are well combined.
5. Let the mixture stand for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to blend.
6. Form the mixture into one-inch balls for appetizers or three-inch ovals for entrees.
7. In the same skillet, heat the remaining oil and butter over medium heat.
8. Sauté the meatballs, turning occasionally, until they are browned on all sides.
9. Transfer the cooked meatballs to a serving dish.
10. Pour lemon juice or red wine into the skillet, bring to a boil, and scrape the drippings.
11. Pour the liquid over the meatballs.
12. Grind fresh black pepper over the top.
13. Sprinkle lightly with fresh minced oregano (and optionally chopped fresh mint or spinach).
